message:
perf(types,btree): zero-copy record serialize primitives into page slot (OPT-A2)

Profile showed Arc::<[u8]>::make_mut 1.77% + __memmove_avx_unaligned_erms
6.77% self-time on INSERT hot path.  The record bytes went
`record_scratch: Vec<u8> -> page buffer memcpy`, meaning one INSERT paid
for three allocations and two bulk memcpys: serialize into scratch,
memcpy into staged page, and a CoW clone (`Arc::make_mut`) on first
shared observation.

This commit lands the primitives needed to serialize record bytes
directly into a pre-carved slot inside the page buffer, eliminating the
intermediate `record_scratch` Vec entirely.  A follow-up commit will
wire them into the direct-simple-INSERT path in connection.rs.

Primitives added:

- `fsqlite-types::record::record_iter_exact_size` (generic) and
  `::record_iter_with_precomputed_header_exact_size` (precomputed-header
  variant): measure exact record byte size without writing, so callers
  can pre-reserve a page slot of the right length.

- `fsqlite-types::record::serialize_record_iter_into_slice` (generic)
  and `::serialize_record_iter_with_precomputed_header_into_slice`
  (precomputed-header variant): write record bytes straight into a
  caller-provided exact-sized `&mut [u8]`.  No intermediate `Vec<u8>`,
  no allocation, no memcpy.

- `fsqlite-btree::cursor::BtCursor::try_append_table_leaf_payload_in_place_no_overflow_(mutate_only_)?with_writer`:
  parallel to the existing `_no_overflow` helper pair, but takes
  `payload_len: usize` plus `writer: FnOnce(&mut [u8]) -> Result<()>`
  instead of a finished payload slice.  The cursor carves the exact
  payload region inside the page, then hands it to the writer which
  serializes in place.

Correctness is enforced by two proptests (512 cases each) asserting
byte-identical output between the Vec-based and slice-based serializers
across arbitrary value sequences, plus five focused unit tests
(empty / all-integer / mixed-type / size-mismatch-rejection /
precomputed-header).

Scope note: the cursor primitives are `#[allow(dead_code)]` pending
the follow-up wire-up commit. They compile, have matching signatures
to the existing `_no_overflow` pair, and reuse the same cell-layout
logic (rowid varint + payload-length varint + in-place payload) — only
the payload-bytes source changes (writer callback vs. &[u8]).

Expected savings once wired: ~1-2% self-time per INSERT from the
`Arc::make_mut` elim + some malloc pressure relief from dropping the
per-statement `record_scratch` round-trip.

Test matrix (rch):
- `cargo check -p fsqlite-core -p fsqlite-btree -p fsqlite-types`: ok
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-types --lib opt_a2`: 5/5 pass
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-types --lib prop_serialize_record`: 2/2 pass (512 cases each)
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-btree --lib`: same 11 failures as baseline (pre-existing, not introduced by this commit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
